threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'café' and not a 'caff'—the locals spot that from a mile away.
Saw a bloke on the 35 last night arguing with his own reflection in the window. No idea what the fight was about, but it got heated.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found my favorite bakery, still open at 7 PM, filled with the fragrant scent of fresh bread, that’s when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you see an impromptu techno party start at 2 AM in a playground, neon lights strung on swings, and locals dancing like it's totally normal.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission when they casually refer to it as ‘the 16th street corridor’ and actually know which taqueria has the best salsa.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full suit give his tie a final tug before hopping onto the bus. Only in SF do you blend boardrooms with street life.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just Gaudí, but the real charm is in the little plazas where locals gossip over coffee, far from those crowded hotspots.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot a recent arrival by their confused expressions at the Gràcia market, especially when trying to haggle with the vendors.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 'quiet night in' means Netflix and a takeaway, not turning up at a warehouse rave after midnight.
Caught a night bus to Brixton last week. A guy painted his nails neon pink while the driver expertly navigated a roundabout—mix of chaos and creativity.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw my neighbor set up a makeshift bar in front of their house during the U-Bahn strike. It was a community party, complete with music and laughter.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find spontaneous street parties erupting over a missed U-Bahn connection. It's like a collective shrug that turns into a celebration.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they can name the BART delays just by the station. Civic Center’s been a mess since that loud bang last week.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling in front of the 16th St. BART while the train screeched to a stop. He just smiled and kept going as riders filed past, totally unfazed.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí but miss the little art studios and secret plazas that give it its real charm.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their reluctance to walk past the Mercat de l'Abaceria with a full shopping bag—it’s a rite of passage here.
